title: Multi-Recorder — Any Editor Can Record Into the Band's Drive
problem: On jam night, the person who created the band may not always be the one with a phone free to record. Any Editor-role band member should be able to hit Record and have the audio land in the band's shared Drive — without requiring each member to set up their own Drive folder or the band creator to be physically present.
analysis:
The existing recording upload flow already makes multi-recorder architecturally trivial: when a member hits Record, the server's /api/upload/init route generates a Google Drive resumable upload session URL using the band creator's stored refresh_token (saved via NextAuth DB adapter). The member's browser then uploads the audio blob directly to the creator's Drive — the member's own Drive is never involved. The audio lands in the correct band Drive folder regardless of who recorded it.

What needs to change is a role-permission gate: currently /api/upload/init checks that the caller is the band creator (Recorder role). Expanding this to allow Editor-role members is the full scope of the server-side change.

On the client: the Record tab in the bottom nav is shown only to Recorder and Editor roles. Member-only users do not see the Record tab.

One critical edge case: the creator's Drive token can expire. The server must attempt a token refresh before generating the upload session URL. If the refresh fails (e.g., creator revoked app access), the error must be surfaced clearly — 'Band creator needs to re-authorize Drive access' — with a notification mechanism to alert the creator.

The resulting clip row in Postgres records both the band creator (driveOwnerEmail) and the actual recording member (recordedByEmail) so the activity feed can correctly show 'Recorded by [member]'.
successCriteria:
- Any Editor-role band member sees the Record tab in the bottom nav and can start a recording
- Member-only role does not see Record tab and cannot access /record
- /api/upload/init: accepts requests from Editor+ roles; fetches and uses band creator's stored refresh_token to generate Drive upload session URL
- Token refresh attempt before generating upload URL; returns clear error if creator's token is invalid: 'Band creator needs to re-authorize Drive access'
- Audio lands in the correct band Drive folder regardless of which Editor recorded
- Clip row stores both driveOwnerEmail (always the band creator) and recordedByEmail (actual recorder)
- Activity feed and clip list show 'Recorded by [name]' when recorder differs from band creator
- If multiple Editors record simultaneously: each gets their own upload session URL; clips created as separate Postgres rows with no conflict
- Post-record screen and upload banner work identically for all recording members
implementation:
1. app/api/upload/init/route.ts: POST — role check `['RECORDER', 'EDITOR'].includes(membership.role)`. Fetches band creator's email from bands table. Queries NextAuth accounts table for creator's access_token + refresh_token. Attempts token refresh via Google OAuth token endpoint if expires_at < now. Generates Drive resumable upload session URL using creator's token. Returns { uploadUrl, clipId, sessionId }.
2. app/api/upload/finalize/route.ts: POST — same role check. Runs FFmpeg transcode on uploaded file. Creates Clip row with recordedByEmail = currentUser.email. Creates v1 ClipVersion row. Updates clip transcodeStatus to DONE.
3. Error handling: if creator token refresh fails, returns 503 with { error: 'CREATOR_TOKEN_INVALID', creatorEmail }. Client shows 'Drive connection needs renewal — ask [creator name] to re-authorize'.
4. components/layout/BottomTabBar.tsx: Record tab hidden when `membership.role === 'MEMBER'`.
5. Prisma schema Clip: recordedByEmail TEXT nullable. clips table stores both fields.
6. components/clips/ClipCard.tsx: shows 'Recorded by X' when recordedByEmail differs from band creator.

decisions.decision: Reuse creator's stored token — no per-member Drive setup
decisions.rationale: drive.file OAuth scope only grants access to files the app created under that specific user's account. A member's token cannot write to the creator's Drive folder. The creator's refresh_token (already stored by NextAuth DB adapter for the audio proxy feature) solves this: the server uses the creator's token to generate the Drive upload session URL, and the member's browser uploads directly to Drive using that URL. No additional OAuth consent or per-member Drive setup needed.
decisions.recommendation: Server fetches creator's tokens from NextAuth accounts table using bandId → band.creatorEmail. Refresh if expired. Generate resumable upload URL with creator's access token. Member's token is not used for Drive operations.

decisions.decision: Editor role can record — Member role cannot
decisions.rationale: Member-only users are passive collaborators (vote, comment). Giving them recording rights would require explaining Drive ownership semantics to every casual invitee. Editors are already trusted with version creation and freeze — recording is a natural extension. The default role for new band invites is Editor, so most band members get recording rights automatically without any explicit configuration.
decisions.recommendation: Role check: `['RECORDER', 'EDITOR'].includes(member.role)`. Record tab hidden for 'MEMBER' role.

decisions.rationale: The clip always lives in the creator's Drive (driveOwnerEmail is always the band creator). The person who pressed Record may be someone else entirely. These are distinct facts: one describes file ownership for Drive API operations, the other describes recording attribution for the activity feed and session history. Conflating them would make it impossible to correctly display 'Recorded by X' or debug Drive issues per-clip.
decisions.recommendation: clips table: add recordedByEmail TEXT column. Set to currentUser.email in /api/upload/finalize. driveOwnerEmail remains the band creator's email. Display as 'Recorded by X' in clip list and activity feed when the two differ.

knownLimitations: If the band creator revokes Google Drive access, ALL recording attempts by any Editor fail until the creator re-authorizes — one token serves the whole band; there is no per-member fallback
Two Editors recording simultaneously produce two separate clip rows with overlapping timestamps — no merging or conflict detection; both appear in the session normally
The creator's refresh_token is stored server-side in the NextAuth accounts table — this security surface already exists for the audio proxy feature; multi-recorder adds no new exposure
userGuide: Any Editor-role band member can record — not just the band creator. Editors see the Record tab in the nav and can start a recording from their own device. The audio automatically lands in the band's Drive folder (owned by the band creator). If the creator's Drive connection expires, a banner appears: 'Drive connection needs renewal — ask [creator name] to re-authorize.' The clip card shows 'Recorded by X' when the recorder is not the band creator.
